medications.indication_rationale: Beta-blockade is the mainstay long-term therapy for Long QT syndrome, but initiation and dosing are a specialist decision after ECG confirmation; it must not be started or dosed over telehealth.
contraindications_in_this_case: Do not initiate/dose remotely - specialist decision after ECG/monitoring confirm the diagnosis

medications.drug_name: QT-prolonging drugs (e.g. certain macrolides, some antiemetics/antidepressants)
medications.indication_rationale: QT-prolonging medications can precipitate Torsades in Long QT syndrome and must be avoided; the patient and future prescribers should be alerted.
contraindications_in_this_case: Avoid all QT-prolonging drugs; flag the risk for any future prescriptions

integrative_alternative_therapies.therapy_name: Treating it as a simple startle-faint and resuming normal activity without assessment
integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Re-triggering (the alarm testing is ongoing) risks a fatal Torsades event; urgent ECG/monitoring and trigger avoidance are required.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Immediately silence all alarms, phones and loud notifications and create a strictly low-stimulus environment; rest quietly and avoid standing/walking until assessed.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Removing the acoustic trigger reduces the risk of a recurrent arrhythmic event before hospital assessment.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Avoid all QT-prolonging medications and inform any future prescriber of the suspected diagnosis.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: QT-prolonging drugs can precipitate fatal Torsades in Long QT syndrome.

patient_education_points.point: Fainting instantly from a loud noise, with brief stiffening and your aunt's young sudden death, suggests an inherited 'electrical' heart-rhythm condition (Long QT). Feeling fine now doesn't remove the danger - a dangerous rhythm could happen again.
Please call 000 for an ambulance now for an urgent heart tracing and monitoring. In the meantime, silence all alarms and phones and keep things quiet, and rest - don't stand or walk around.
If you faint again or collapse and don't respond, your roommate should call 000 and start CPR immediately.
Once confirmed, this is very manageable with specific medication (beta-blockers) and avoiding triggers and certain drugs - but you need the urgent assessment first.

follow_up_plan.interval: Immediate emergency assessment and admission for ECG/monitoring; specialist cardiology/genetics follow-up post-discharge
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: QTc on ECG, monitoring for arrhythmia, echocardiography, genotype/genetic counselling, specialist initiation of beta-blockade and trigger-avoidance/ICD considerations, and family cascade screening
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Any recurrent collapse or unresponsiveness before assessment - call 000 and start CPR if unresponsive.

must_include_items: Recognition of probable inherited arrhythmic syncope (Long QT syndrome), not a simple faint or seizure
Immediate ambulance (000) for urgent ECG and continuous monitoring
Trigger avoidance now - silence alarms/phones, low-stimulus environment, rest
No remote initiation/dosing of beta-blocker; avoid QT-prolonging drugs
acceptable_alternatives: 'Inherited heart-rhythm condition', 'Long QT syndrome', 'dangerous electrical heart problem' are acceptable framings
Immediate ambulance is preferred; urgent same-day ED is the minimum acceptable if the patient can be safely transported
errors_of_omission: Family history of young sudden death not elicited/weighted
errors_of_commission: Reassuring the patient it was a simple startle-faint
Misdiagnosing it as epilepsy and deferring cardiac assessment
Advising a next-day GP visit despite the ongoing trigger and risk
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: CRITICAL CASE: A pass requires (1) recognising probable Long QT / arrhythmic syncope and arranging immediate ECG/monitoring (ambulance), (2) trigger avoidance, AND (3) CPR safety-netting. Clearing this as a benign faint or misdiagnosing epilepsy without cardiac assessment is a dangerous, automatic-fail under-triage given the sudden-death risk.
